claims.claim: ParallelPipeline runs N sub-pipelines concurrently, each wrapped in its own source/sink
claims.found: class ParallelPipeline(BasePipeline) at line 24; loop at 59-74 creates PipelineSource (67) and PipelineSink (70) per branch, 'a source before the pipeline and a sink after so we control the frames'

claims.claim: Frontmatter code_ref :1857 anchors preemptive generation / on_preemptive_generation
claims.found: Line 1857 is 'self._interrupt_by_audio_activity()' inside a final-transcript handler. on_preemptive_generation actually starts at line 1872.

claims.claim: on_preemptive_generation fires _generate_reply(... schedule_speech=False) on interim transcript, gated by max_speech_duration and max_retries; _cancel_preemptive_generation scraps it
claims.found: _cancel_preemptive_generation() at 1883; max_speech_duration check 1887; max_retries check 1891; _generate_reply(... schedule_speech=False) at 1903-1909. All present within 1872-1919 (note's anchor 1857 is off by ~15 lines but the cited range covers the mechanism).

claims.claim: Three streams: Audio Input (20ms chunks VAD/preprocess/buffer), Transcription (streaming partials), Response Generation (LLM on partials + predict-and-scrap)
claims.found: Lines 20-33 of vapi-pipeline-part-1.md: '20ms chunks', VAD/Audio Preprocessing/Real-time Buffering (24-27), 'Streaming STT providing partial results' with the I-need-to example (30), endpointing predicts user finished + 'predict and scrap' (32-33).

claims.claim: predict-and-scrap = greedy inference; 'the user never hears the scrapped attempt'
claims.found: vapi-pipeline-part-2.md line 47: 'Greedy Inference: ... If wrong and they continue, instantly cancel and restart ... The user never hears the scrapped attempt.'

claims.claim: ElevenLabs: predicting end-of-speech 'results in multiple LLM generation requests with the same conversation context within a single turn'
claims.found: Line 17: 'predicting when a user has finished speaking. In some cases this results in multiple LLM generation requests with the same conversation context within a single turn.'

claims.claim: Modal: VAD-gated segment-then-transcribe (Parakeet) beat streaming STT on final-transcript time; 'the only thing that matters for total v2v latency is the final transcript time'
claims.found: Line 33: 'using Pipecat's local VAD + turn detection to segment audio and passing that to Parakeet was FASTER than ... streaming STT' and 'the only thing that matters for total voice-to-voice latency is the final transcript time'.

claims.claim: Cross-note links assert neighbors (LAT-02 speculative-generation, TURN-02 endpointing) corroborate
claims.found: Note lists links as related, does not assert a specific proof from a neighbor; not spot-checked.

summary: All source figures/quotes (vapi-pipeline-1/2, ElevenLabs, Modal) verified verbatim. Pipecat ParallelPipeline code_ref accurate. One MISCITE: frontmatter code_ref agent_activity.py:1857 points at _interrupt_by_audio_activity(); on_preemptive_generation actually starts at line 1872 (the prose range 1857-1919 still covers the mechanism, which is fully present). No WRONG claims.
